label: Debugging / Testing
title: Debugging, testing, and hot reload
lead: Speed up iteration with logs, tutorial-friendly config toggles, console harnesses, and CUCoreLib's strict content reload flow.
bodyHtml:
Fast mod-testing loop

Most CUCoreLib iteration gets faster when you separate startup-only work from content registration. Keep console commands, Harmony patches, and other one-time setup in Awake(), but move content into split methods like LoadAssets(), RegisterItems(), RegisterLiquids(), and RegisterRecipes().

That split helps even before hot reload enters the picture: it keeps your mod easier to reason about, gives you narrow places to debug, and lines up with the strict CUCoreLib content reload flow.

  1. Build the mod.
  2. Watch the BepInEx console or BepInEx/LogOutput.log.
  3. Use temporary console commands to spawn or verify exactly what you changed.
  4. If the mod follows the strict content shape, rebuild and run reloadcontent <modGuid> in singleplayer.

Strict content reload

CUCoreLib's built-in DLL reload path is intentionally narrow. It is singleplayer only and only reloads item, liquid, recipe, and locale/text content. It does not rerun Awake(), and it does not support buildings, save providers, moodles, Harmony setup, mod options, or multiplayer registration.

reloadcontent com.example.mymod
listhotreload
reloaddll com.example.mymod
  • reloadcontent <modGuid> loads the rebuilt DLL, clears the previous owner-scoped content, and replays only recognized content methods.
  • listhotreload shows which loaded mods are compatible, which methods were recognized, and the selected DLL source path.
  • reloaddll is now a strict-mode help/inspection command rather than a generic runtime assembly swap.

The preferred entrypoint contract is now an explicit attribute on parameterless methods, so mods are not forced to use specific method names.

[ContentReloadEntry(ContentReloadEntryStage.LoadAssets)]
private void LoadAcidAssets() { ... }

[ContentReloadEntry(ContentReloadEntryStage.RegisterItems)]
private void RegisterAcidItems() { ... }

[ContentReloadEntry(ContentReloadEntryStage.RegisterRecipes, Order = 10)]
private void RegisterLateRecipes() { ... }

Methods are replayed by stage, then by optional Order. Mods that want strict content reload support must opt in with [ContentReloadEntry(...)] on parameterless methods.

Watch mode and override DLL paths

Strict content reload also has an optional watch loop. It is configured per mod in BepInEx/config/CUCoreLib/ContentReload.json.

{
  "PollIntervalSeconds": 2,
  "DebounceMilliseconds": 1200,
  "Mods": {
    "com.example.mymod": {
      "OverrideDllPath": "C:/Users/you/source/MyMod/bin/Debug/net48/MyMod.dll",
      "WatchEnabled": true
    }
  }
}

Use an override path when the loaded plugin DLL is not the file you are actively rebuilding. Watch mode compares timestamp and size first, then hashes when needed, so it avoids doing heavy work every poll.
